query: How do I make this incremental pipeline safe to rerun after failures and backfills?
We keep getting duplicate rows after retrying a CDC job. What idempotency pattern should we use?
Should this warehouse load use overwrite, merge, or transaction semantics?
How should we structure raw, intermediate, and mart layers for our analytics platform?
Should we use an RDW, MDW, or lakehouse for our analytics roadmap?
Help me design dbt tests so a mart only publishes when row counts and core metrics look sane.
